description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three mushrooms with large, rounded caps that are bright red and covered in irregular white spots. The caps are smooth and slightly domed, with the largest mushroom positioned centrally and two smaller mushrooms grouped closely at its base. The stems are thick, cylindrical, and pale beige, tapering slightly as they meet the ground. The white spots on the caps are unevenly distributed, giving the mushrooms a distinctive, eye-catching appearance reminiscent of the Amanita muscaria species. The underside of the caps is not visible, so the presence of gills cannot be confirmed. The mushrooms appear to be growing together as a group, likely intended to evoke a whimsical or naturalistic element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A cluster of three red mushrooms with white spots and thick beige stems, featuring smooth, domed caps. The group is visually striking and reminiscent of classic toadstools.
description_view_0: The largest mushroom cap dominates the view, with two smaller mushrooms visible at its base and the white spots clearly distributed across all caps.
description_view_1: Frontal view showing all three mushroom caps, with the central large cap flanked by the two smaller ones, and the stems mostly visible.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the overlapping arrangement of the mushrooms, with the caps' curvature and spot patterns prominent.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the largest cap, showing the full pattern of white spots and only hints of the smaller mushrooms beneath.
